calendar.matters:
PETITION Petition To Appoint C. H. Johnson As Guardian Ad Litem For Principal For Review If Trust Accounts A
Off Calendar Aug-01-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter called with case number PCN-11-295191 Conservatorship of William Fries.

Appearances: Seth Skootsky for Michael Roosevelt and Wells Fargo Bank (Co-trustees). Jerry Hallisey for Charlene Johnson. Debra Fox for Thomas Lucas (Proposed Conservator).

Mr. Hallisey is appearing for Ms. Johnson because she is ill. The Court questioned the need of a conservatorship with the Trust in place and the Power of Attoney in place and according to Ms. Johnson's last report, Mr. Fries no longer has concerns with regard to the Trust. Mr. Skootsky presented oral argument to support his position with regard the need of the Guardian Ad Litem reviewing the trust matters, and expressed concern of conflict of interest with Mr. Wulff as agent with Power of Attorney and as employee for Wells Fargo Bank. Ms. Fox stated the conservatorship petition can go off calendar. The Court granted the Petition to Appoint Ms. Johnson as Guradian Ad Litem for Mr. Fries with respect to trust matter but not imposing additional duties or court oversight (subject to a revised order). Mr. Skootsky's request to have Mr. Wolff included in the order is not granted. Mr. Skootsky is instructed to prepare a revised order.

Judge: Peter Busch. Court Investigator: Cynthia Jones. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.
